subject: Re: Resolution stuck
body:
Hi Alister

Thank you for your quick reply! I will try it.

One thing still unclear for me is the temporal sampling.

As far as I understand, the temporal sampling only influence particle pose 
(translational and rotational).. Does it influence CTF refinement and tilt 
movies refinement as I asked above?

Best,
Mingyu

2025년 6월 15일 일요일 오후 11시 3분 31초 UTC+9에 alist...@gmail.com님이 작성:

> Hi Mingyu,
>
> You’re fitting too many parameter, your data likely aren’t strong enough 
> to support this. 
>
> Start with 1 temporal pose and fit only 1x1 image shift for a while - then 
> add more and more parameters once resolution plateaus 
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jun 15, 2025, at 00:35, Mingyu Jung <jmg...@snu.ac.kr> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hi all,
>
>
>
> I have a problem with improving resolution during M refinement. 
>
> After subtomogram extraction in Warp and 3D refinement in Relion, I 
> achieved 7.4 A and refine my particles with M.
> Box size is 312. Image type is mrc with 10 fractions and temporal sampling 
> for pose is 3.
>
> At first I have a significant increase in resolution with pose, image warp 
> (4 x 4), tilt angles and volume warp (3 x 3 x 2 x 10) refinement and 
> achieved 6.7 A map.
>
> However, tilt movie and CTF refinement did not improve the resolution. I 
> used species with better than 6.7A as CTF refinement.
>
> If you have any experience or suggestions regarding this issue, please let 
> me know.
>
> Best,
> Mingyu
>
>
>
> --

> "Warp" group.

> .
>
>
